name: Culvert Opps

seoDescription: Culvert Opps Construction (West Coast ADU) builds accessory dwelling units, manufactured homes, and fire-rebuild homes across Sonoma County and the North Bay. Licensed CA general building contractor, Lic. #804241.

sections.heading: Building homes when they're needed most
body: Culvert Opps Construction got its start in the aftermath of the 2015 Northern California fires, when it was among the first to step in and help families who had lost their homes. Providing fast, dependable housing became the company's mission, and it has shaped how the team works ever since.

Operating as West Coast ADU, the company designs, permits, and builds accessory dwelling units and manufactured homes across Sonoma County and the wider North Bay. Owner Darron Peter and his crew handle the work from the ground up — excavation, foundations, and demolition through to the finished, move-in-ready home, including the crane sets that drop a manufactured unit onto its new foundation.
highlights: ADUs, manufactured homes, and fire rebuilds
Serving Sonoma and Marin counties since the 2015 North Bay fires

image.alt: Culvert Opps crew building the deck on a new metal-clad ADU

sections.heading: Your ADU, step by step
sections.intro: The path from first call to move-in is straightforward — here's how a project runs with us.

items.title: Consultation & site inspection
items.body: We meet at your property, look at the lot, and give you a free estimate for what's possible.

items.title: Select your ADU design
items.body: Choose from hundreds of floor plans and sizes to fit your lot, budget, and how you'll use the space.

items.title: Permitting
items.body: We and our trusted partners handle the building, electrical, and zoning permits so you don't have to.

items.title: Site prep & construction
items.body: Excavation, foundation, the crane set, and finish work — all the way to a move-in-ready home.

items.a: An ADU (Accessory Dwelling Unit) is a smaller, secondary residential unit on the same lot as a primary single-family home — a backyard cottage, a garage conversion, an attached addition, or an interior unit.

items.q: How much does it cost to build an ADU?
items.a: On average, building an ADU runs from about $50,000 to $350,000 or more, depending on size and finishes. That range typically includes design, permitting, construction, and utility connections — we'll give you a real estimate after a site visit.

items.q: Do I need a permit to build an ADU?
items.a: Yes — an ADU typically requires building, electrical, and sometimes zoning or land-use permits. We and our trusted partners handle the permitting for you.
Generally the process takes anywhere from several months to a year, from planning through completion.

sections.heading: Let's talk about your project
sections.body: Call (707) 236-7624 or message us for a free consultation and site inspection in Santa Rosa.

sections.heading: Building homes when they're needed most
body: Culvert Opps Construction got its start in the aftermath of the 2015 Northern California fires, when it was among the first to step in and help families who had lost their homes. Providing fast, dependable housing became the company's mission, and it has shaped how the team works ever since.

Operating as West Coast ADU, the company designs, permits, and builds accessory dwelling units and manufactured homes across Sonoma County and the wider North Bay. Owner Darron Peter and his crew handle the work from the ground up — excavation, foundations, and demolition through to the finished, move-in-ready home, including the crane sets that drop a manufactured unit onto its new foundation.

Culvert Opps is a licensed California general building contractor (Lic. #804241) and has pulled permits on more than 50 projects across the region in recent years, with a focus on ADUs, manufactured dwellings, and rebuilding after fire.
